---
title: Examples demonstrating Parquet utility functions
---

This page groups together examples for the Parquet utility helpers:
`pq_list_files()`, `pq_last_modified()`, `pq_archive()`, `pq_restore()`, and
`pq_remove()`.

The goal is different from the API reference. The API pages document arguments
and return values. This page shows how the utilities fit together in the
practical workflow of maintaining a local Parquet repository.

## When to use this page

Use these helpers when you already have a Parquet repository and want to:

- inspect what is in it
- check which vintage of a table you currently have
- archive a current active file before replacing it
- restore an older archived vintage
- remove active or archived files explicitly

The related API pages are:

- [pq_list_files](reference/pq_list_files.qmd)
- [pq_last_modified](reference/pq_last_modified.qmd)
- [pq_archive](reference/pq_archive.qmd)
- [pq_restore](reference/pq_restore.qmd)
- [pq_remove](reference/pq_remove.qmd)

## Setup

The executable examples below assume access to a local Parquet repository with
at least one known schema and table. Adjust these values to match your local
setup before rendering if needed.

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-setup
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
import os
from pathlib import Path

from dotenv import find_dotenv, load_dotenv

dotenv_path = find_dotenv(usecwd=True)
load_dotenv(dotenv_path or None, override=True)

from db2pq import pq_last_modified, pq_list_files
```

## Inspect a schema directory

Start by listing the Parquet tables available in a schema.

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-list-schema
#| warning: false
pq_list_files("comp")
```

If you want to inspect the archive directory instead:

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-list-archive
#| warning: false
pq_list_files("comp", archive=True)
```

If your project uses a repository outside the default `DATA_DIR`, pass
`data_dir` explicitly.

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-list-explicit-dir
#| warning: false
pq_list_files("ff", data_dir="~/Dropbox/pq_data")
```

## Check the current active vintage

Use `pq_last_modified()` to inspect the embedded metadata on the active file.

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-active-vintage
#| warning: false
pq_last_modified(table_name="dsf", schema="crsp")
```

This is often the fastest way to confirm what vintage a local Parquet file
represents before starting analysis.

You can also inspect a specific file path directly:

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-explicit-file
#| warning: false
dsf_v2 = Path.home() / "Dropbox/pq_data/crsp/dsf_v2.parquet"

pq_last_modified(file_name=dsf_v2)
```

## Inspect archived vintages

If you archive replaced files, you can ask for the archived versions of a
table:

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-archived-vintages
#| warning: false
pq_last_modified(table_name="funda", schema="comp", archive=True)
```

That returns a table-like summary of the archived vintages for the requested
dataset.

To inspect all archived files for a schema:

```{python}
#| label: parquet-utils-schema-archive-summary
#| warning: false
pq_last_modified(schema="comp", archive=True)
```

## Archive the currently active file

You can archive a file manually even outside an update workflow.

```{python}
#| eval: true
from db2pq import pq_archive

pq_archive(table_name="funda", schema="comp")
```

Or archive an exact file path:

```{python}
#| eval: true
funda = Path.home() / "Dropbox/pq_data/comp/funda.parquet"

funda_archive = pq_archive(file_name=funda)
funda_archive
```

```{python}
#| eval: true
#| include: false
from db2pq import pq_restore 
from pathlib import Path

pq_restore(Path(funda_archive).stem, "comp", 
           data_dir=Path.home() / "Dropbox/pq_data")
```


This is useful when you want to preserve the current active vintage before
running an experimental refresh or downstream transformation.

## Restore an archived vintage

To promote an archived file back into the active schema directory:

```{python}
#| eval: true
from db2pq import pq_restore

archive_files = pq_list_files("comp", archive=True)

if archive_files:
    pq_restore(archive_files[0], "comp")
```

The archived basename may include or omit the `.parquet` suffix.

If an active destination file already exists, `pq_restore()` archives that file
first by default before restoring the archived vintage.

## Remove a file explicitly

Use `pq_remove()` when you want to delete an active or archived file rather
than archive it.

```{python}
#| eval: true
from db2pq import pq_remove

pq_remove(table_name="dsi", schema="crsp")
```

Of course, I probably want that file, so let me use `wrds_update_pq()` to recover it!

```{python}
#| eval: true
from db2pq import wrds_update_pq

wrds_update_pq(table_name="dsi", schema="crsp")
```

To remove an archived file:

```{python}
#| eval: false
pq_remove(
    table_name="funda_20260331T060000Z",
    schema="comp",
    archive=True,
)
```

Or remove a file by exact path:

```{python}
funda_archive
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
pq_remove(file_name=funda_archive)
```

## Related pages

- [Data management ideas](data-management.qmd)
- [WRDS to Parquet](wrds-to-pq.qmd)
- [PostgreSQL to Parquet](pg-to-pq.qmd)
- [API Reference](reference/index.qmd)
